case_title: DAVID SCHENDEL VS. JONATHAN DENNY et al

REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT DENNY, JONATHAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 8, 2013 TO GIVE MR. DENNY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND 2.6C. MR. DENNY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS AND THE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 9, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE OVERRULED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. "ONE WHO WILLFULLY CONSUMES ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES TO THE POINT OF INTOXICATION, KNOWING THAT HE THEREAFTER MUST OPERATE A MOTOR VEHICLE, THEREBY COMBINING SHARPLY IMPAIRED PHYSICAL AND MENTAL FACULTIES WITH A VEHICLE CAPABLE OF GREAT FORCE AND SPEED, REASONABLY MAY BE HELD TO EXHIBIT A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS." TAYLOR V. SUP. CRT. (1979) 24 CAL.3D 890, 897. THE BALANCE OF THE MOTION, PERTAINING TO LINES 6 AND 7 OF "ATTACHMENT 4" TO THE COMPLAINT, IS DENIED AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER ON THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT CONTENDS, AMONG OTHER THINGS, THAT THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE. IN THE COURSE OF AMENDING THE COMPLAINT FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE, PLAINTIFF MAY ALSO AMEND THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AT PLAINTIFF'S DISCRETION. THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FACTS, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Continued To Oct-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer to the third cause of action for general negligence is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant contends, among other things, that the third cause of action is duplicative of the first cause of action for general negligence. In the course of amending the complaint for general negligence, Plaintiff may also amend the first cause of action for general negligence at Plaintiff's discretion. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend facts, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, to state a cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for exemplary damages. "One who willfully consumes alcoholic beverages to the point of intoxication, knowing that he thereafter must operate a motor vehicle, thereby combining sharply impaired physical and mental faculties with a vehicle capable of great force and speed, reasonably may be held to exhibit a conscious disregard of the safety of others." Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897. The balance of the motion, pertaining to lines 6 and 7 of "attachment 4" to the Complaint, is denied as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
